settings.text:
TDLR: The fabrics used for this piece are historic in nature, and may have unique idiosyncrasies. We recommend spot cleaning, hand washing cold, and air drying. Color may transfer. Isaboko is not liable for damage.

History: For centuries kimonos were washed only seasonally by specialists using a meticulous process. The garment is completely unstitched, sewn into a long roll, then hand-washed, pressed, and reassembled.

Cleaning option 1 *my favorite*: airing your garment out between wearings and spot cleaning as necessary. Fill a spray bottle with vodka and spray gently from the inside of the garment in order to kill bacteria and remove odors. Using a steamer is another option to help keep pieces in good shape.

If you really must you can dry clean it.

Please email us with questions info@isaboko.com
